name: Cartesia (Ultra-Fast Real-Time Text-to-Speech)
pricing_notes: Pay-as-you-go: $0.065/1000 chars; $15 free credits on signup

performance.notes: ~90ms TTFB (time to first byte); real-time streaming; fastest commercial TTS

agent_readiness.notes: Ultra-low latency TTS for real-time voice applications. Sonic model family. WebSocket streaming. ~90ms TTFB — fastest latency in industry. Voice cloning with 3s sample. 40+ languages. Emotion/speed/pitch control. PCM/raw/WAV output. LiveKit integration. Works with OpenAI real-time API workflows. Python and JavaScript SDKs. Used by voice AI startups, real-time conversational AI, phone agents. Founded by ex-CMU/Berkeley researchers.

gotchas: Newer startup — reliability track record still building
Higher cost than ElevenLabs for basic use cases
Voice cloning quality varies with sample quality
best_when: Real-time conversational AI requiring sub-100ms TTS latency
Voice agents and phone bots needing streaming TTS
Applications where latency is more important than voice variety
LiveKit or WebRTC voice pipeline integration
avoid_when: Static audio generation without latency requirements (ElevenLabs better)
Large voice library needed (ElevenLabs has more voices)
summary: Cartesia offers ultra-low latency TTS (~90ms TTFB) for real-time voice applications. Sonic model family with WebSocket streaming. Voice cloning from 3s samples. 40+ languages. Emotion/speed control. Python and JavaScript SDKs. Fastest commercial TTS latency — ideal for real-time conversational AI, voice agents, and phone bots. ElevenLabs for voice variety; Cartesia for minimum latency.
